summary: I was born in Lakeland, Florida, 24 years ago, though I was just a little girl then. Lakeland is part of my roots, and it’s where my early life began before my family moved to a farm near the shore. Being born there makes me a real Florida Crooker, and I take pride in that identity.

My daddy was born in Polk County, Florida, which is where part of my family heritage comes from. Polk County is important to us because it’s where my father’s side of the family has lived for a long time. It’s close to where I was born and raised, and it connects us to our Florida farming roots.

My mother was born in Lake County, Florida, so that’s where her side of the family comes from. Lake County is part of my family’s history, and I feel connected to it through my mother’s English stock background. It’s another piece of the Florida story that shapes who I am.

Florida has been home to my family for generations, ever since my great-great-grandfather came here from Virginia after arriving from Ireland. I was born and raised here, and I consider myself a true Floridian. My life, my family, and all our farming efforts are deeply tied to this state.

My great-great-grandfather originally came from Virginia before he moved to Florida, so that’s where some of our family roots trace back to. Though we’ve been in Florida ever since, Virginia is part of our ancestral story. It connects us to the broader history of our family’s migration.

Our family history says that my great-great-grandfather came from Ireland before settling in Virginia and then moving to Florida. Ireland is where our earliest known ancestors came from, and it’s a place I’ve always been curious about. I wish I had the means to trace our family history more thoroughly there.

On my mother’s side, our English stock is said to come from a place called Birmingham. I’ve always wondered about Birmingham and the stories behind it, but none of us know many definite details. It’s part of the mystery and pride in my family’s English heritage.

Frank’s family is all from Georgia. He was born near Oakland, Florida, but his roots are Georgian, and his family now lives in Norwood, Georgia. His daddy is superintendent of a peach orchard there, and Frank talks about possibly moving to Georgia to help with the farm work, though I prefer to stay here in Florida.

Frank was born near Oakland, Florida, which ties him to that area even though he was raised here in Florida. Oakland is part of his early life story and connects him to Georgia through his family’s farming background. It’s a place that marks the beginning of his journey before settling here.

Frank’s family now lives in Norwood, Florida, where his daddy manages a peach orchard and has a half interest in a large farm. Norwood is where Frank’s family farming life is centered, and it’s the place he might take us to help with the work. It represents the agricultural life that Frank is connected to through his family.

I once made a lot of artificial flowers for the Girl Scout Minstrel, which was a new kind of work for me but something I enjoyed. It was around Christmas when I also helped a florist make pine wreaths, and that was my first time doing that kind of craft. Being involved with the Girl Scouts gave me opportunities to use my skills and earn a little money with odd jobs.
